human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for franchise-specific analytics boundaries, FDD and Item 19 or Item 20 record awareness, multi-unit structure handling, royalty and POS reconciliation behavior, disclosure-safe refusal posture, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to franchise analytics, FDD and Item 19 or Item 20 aware metric logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover the FTC Franchise Rule and compliance guide, NASAA franchise guidelines and commentaries, NASAA EFD workflow, SEC KPI disclosure guidance, and recognized IFA franchise terminology.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported earnings and causal claims, multi-unit analysis, royalty-boundary escalation, missing facts, anti-generic checks, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/franchise-systems-and-multi-unit-enterprise-support/franchise-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from franchise compliance, royalty administration, franchise operations, field consulting, franchise development support, and other adjacent lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated franchise analytics specialist for metric-definition, royalty and POS reconciliation, multi-unit reporting, Item 19 aware caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
